message:
fix(daemon): resolve scanner deadlock that caused 0 scans on all production machines

Three root causes fixed:

1. Walker work queue deadlock (root cause): process_directory() used blocking
   send() on bounded work channel (cap 1024). When both worker threads were
   inside process_directory on large directory trees, the queue filled and
   both blocked — classic producer-consumer deadlock. Fixed by using
   send_timeout(100ms) with skip fallback.

2. Scanner entry loop hung forever: `for entry in rx` blocked indefinitely
   when walker threads deadlocked (channel never closed). The 60s budget
   check was inside the loop body and only fired on new entries. Fixed by
   replacing with recv_timeout(2s) loop so budget fires even with no entries.

3. Scanner→executor blocking send: del_tx.send() could block the scanner
   thread if the executor channel (cap 64) was full. Changed to try_send()
   with drop-and-log fallback.

Also fixes pre-existing clippy errors (Rust 1.93):
- io::Error::new(Other, ...) → io::Error::other()
- Redundant .clone() on moved values in config defaults
- Dead test placeholder doc comment

Verified: 1781 lib tests pass, clippy clean, deployed to TRJ — first scan
completed successfully (was stuck at 0 scans for 9+ hours before fix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
